course_title: Advanced clinical practicum in sports and exercise physiotherapy

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate advanced clinical reasoning, clinical skills and exercise testing and programming in the assessment, differential diagnoses and management of clients with musculoskeletal and sports injuries and conditions.
Demonstrate advanced treatment planning and goal setting for immediate and ongoing management to optimise the client's function for work, exercise and sport performance.
Demonstrate high-level understanding of client needs, inter-professional practice and indications for referral in the management of clients with musculoskeletal and sports injuries and conditions.
Integrate feedback and self-reflect on strengths and weaknesses in clinical skills and reasoning in a sports and exercise physiotherapy clinical environment.

assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 13/11/2026

The Progressive Clinical Assessment will occur throughout the clinical placement block. The Progressive Clinical Assessment dates for each placement block are:

Block 1: July 27-August 7

Block 2: August 17-28

Block 3: November 2-13
assessments.task_description:
Throughout the clinical placement block, students will be assessed on the following: Clinical Performance, Feedback Action Planning and Professional Participation and Contribution.

Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Given this assessment is related to a placement with clinical information please see more information on use of AI in student placements under additional assessment information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
The Clinical Consultation VIVAs will be due/completed at the end of the first (Initial Consultation VIVA) and second (Follow-up Consultation VIVA) week of the clinical placement block. The Clinical Consultation VIVA due dates for each placement block are:

Block 1 (July 27-August 7)

Initial Consultation VIVA: Thursday July 30th to Friday July 31st

Follow-up Consultation VIVA: Thursday August 6th to Friday August 7th

Block 2: August 17-28

Initial Consultation VIVA: Thursday August 20th to Friday August 21st

Follow-up Consultation VIVA: Thursday August 27th to Friday August 28th

Block 3: November 2-13

Initial Consultation VIVA: Thursday November 5th to Friday November 6th

Follow-up Consultation VIVA: Thursday November 12th to Friday November 13th
assessments.task_description:
Throughout the placement, students will be examined on two clinical consultation VIVAs. 

Initial Consultation VIVA (25%)

Students will be examined on their performance of an initial consultation with a physiotherapy patient (including assessment and treatment).

Follow-up Consultation VIVA (25%)

Students will be examined on their performance of a follow-up treatment session (2nd or 3rd session) with a physiotherapy client. Prior to this assessment, students must prepare a written report about the assessment and treatment of the patient to date in the format outlined on Blackboard. Following the treatment session, the student will be asked questions from the examiner.

The total mark for the two Clinical Consultation VIVAs must be a passing grade (60% or higher) to pass the course. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. 

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a Clinical Reasoning Form for a patient they have seen in their clinical caseload (note this is a separate patient to those used in their peer-coaching sessions).

Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Given this assessment is related to a placement with clinical information please see more information on use of AI in student placements under additional assessment information.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Clinical Reasoning Peer Coaching

Students are required to complete two Clinical Reasoning Forms for patients they have seen in their clinical practice. They will meet with their assigned peer-coach on two occasions (for approximately 1 hour each) to discuss their clinical reasoning (guided by the Clinical Reasoning Form) and answer reflective questions asked by their peer-coach. They will also be a peer-coach where they will meet with and listen to their peer present their clinical reasoning (guided by the Clinical Reasoning Form) for two of their patients (each meeting will be approximately 1 hour) and ask questions to facilitate their reflection on their clinical reasoning.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Clinical Reasoning Peer Coaching Students are required to complete two Clinical Reasoning Forms for patients they have seen in their clinical practice. They will meet with their assigned peer-coach on two occasions (for approximately 1 hour each) to discuss their clinical reasoning (guided by the Clinical Reasoning Form) and answer reflective questions asked by their peer-coach. They will also be a peer-coach where they will meet with and listen to their peer present their clinical reasoning (guided by the Clinical Reasoning Form) for two of their patients (each meeting will be approximately 1 hour) and ask questions to facilitate their reflection on their clinical reasoning.

learning_activities.topic:
Clinical Placement

You will undertake a 2-week clinical placement in Brisbane with trained clinical educators/mentors who will help to develop your clinical reasoning and skills. The clinical placement contains clinical supervision/mentoring, as well as activities to further develop your clinical and self-reflection. During the clinical placement, you will use Clinical Reasoning Forms and Feedback Action Planning Forms to facilitate your development

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
